title: The Necklace
author: Guy de Maupassant
short_description: A woman borrows a diamond necklace for one evening of social glory — loses it — and spends ten years in poverty replacing it.
description: Mathilde Loisel sacrifices her entire youth and beauty replacing what she believes was a real diamond necklace — only to learn at the end that it was paste. The most perfectly sprung ironic twist in short fiction history.
benefits: Read a legally cleared digital text.
Enjoy a clean, structured reading format.
Access a render-safe ebook prepared for web reading.
who_for: Users who prefer structured digital reading
learnings: Understand the major themes and structure of the work.
Engage with the text in a clean digital format.
about_author: Guy de Maupassant (1850–1893) was a French master of the short story, celebrated for his psychological precision, ironic twists, and portraits of Norman and Parisian society.
rights_basis: Guy de Maupassant died 1893; first published 1884. Public domain in India and the U.S.
